From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Medal awarded for achieving second place

"Silver Medalist" redirects here. For the 2009 Chinese film, see [Silver Medalist (film)](/wiki/Silver_Medalist_(film) "Silver Medalist (film)").

Not to be confused with [Silver Award](/wiki/Silver_Award_(disambiguation) "Silver Award (disambiguation)"), [Silver Star](/wiki/Silver_Star_(disambiguation) "Silver Star (disambiguation)"), [silver metal](/wiki/Silver "Silver"), or [Silver Medallion](/wiki/Silver_Medallion_(disambiguation) "Silver Medallion (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/59/1896_Olympic_medal.jpg)](/wiki/File:1896_Olympic_medal.jpg)

The [1896 Olympic](/wiki/1896_Olympics "1896 Olympics") silver medal

A **silver medal**, in sports and other similar areas involving competition, is a [medal](/wiki/Medal "Medal") made of, or [plated with](/wiki/Silver_plating "Silver plating"), [silver](/wiki/Silver "Silver") awarded to the second-place finisher, or [runner-up](/wiki/Runner-up "Runner-up"), of contests or competitions such as the [Olympic Games](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games"), [Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games "Commonwealth Games"), etc. The outright winner receives a [gold medal](/wiki/Gold_medal "Gold medal") and the third place a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal"). More generally, silver is traditionally a metal sometimes used for all types of high-quality medals, including artistic ones.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Sports")]

### Olympic Games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Olympic Games")]

Main article: [Olympic medal](/wiki/Olympic_medal "Olympic medal")

During the first Olympic event in [1896](/wiki/1896_Summer_Olympics "1896 Summer Olympics"), number one achievers or winners' medals were in fact made of [silver](/wiki/Silver "Silver") metal. The custom of [gold](/wiki/Gold "Gold")-silver-[bronze](/wiki/Bronze "Bronze") for the first three places dates from the [1904 games](/wiki/1904_Summer_Olympics "1904 Summer Olympics") and has been copied for many other sporting events. [Minting](/wiki/Mint_(coin) "Mint (coin)") the medals is the responsibility of the host city. From [1928](/wiki/1928_Summer_Olympics "1928 Summer Olympics") to [1968](/wiki/1968_Summer_Olympics "1968 Summer Olympics") the design was always the same: the obverse showed a generic design by [Florentine](/wiki/Florence "Florence") artist [Giuseppe Cassioli](/wiki/Giuseppe_Cassioli "Giuseppe Cassioli") with text giving the host city; the reverse showed another generic design of an Olympic champion. From [1972](/wiki/1972_Summer_Olympics "1972 Summer Olympics")–[2000](/wiki/2000_Summer_Olympics "2000 Summer Olympics"), Cassioli's design (or a slight reworking) remained on the obverse with a custom design by the host city on the reverse. Noting that Cassioli's design showed a [Roman amphitheatre](/wiki/Roman_amphitheatre "Roman amphitheatre") for what was originally a Greek games, a new obverse design was commissioned for the [Athens](/wiki/2004_Summer_Olympics "2004 Summer Olympics") Games. [Winter Olympics](/wiki/Winter_Olympics "Winter Olympics") medals have been of more varied design.

### The Open Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Open Championship")]

In [The Open Championship](/wiki/The_Open_Championship "The Open Championship") golf tournament, the Silver Medal is an award presented to the lowest scoring [amateur](/wiki/Amateurism "Amateurism") player at the tournament.

### Rejection of silver medals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Rejection of silver medals")]

See also: [Bronze medal § Psychological study](/wiki/Bronze_medal#Psychological_study "Bronze medal")

In many sports with an elimination tournament, including those with a [third place playoff](/wiki/Third_place_playoff "Third place playoff") (such as Olympic ice hockey, Olympic soccer, [FIFA World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup "FIFA World Cup")), silver is the only medal given to a team that loses its final game, whereas gold and bronze are earned by teams winning their final matches.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Notable athletes such as [Jocelyne Larocque](/wiki/Jocelyne_Larocque "Jocelyne Larocque") (2018 Olympics) removed their runners-up/silver medals right after receiving them; Larocque was later ordered by the [International Ice Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Ice_Hockey_Federation "International Ice Hockey Federation") official to put her silver medal back on.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Military and government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Military and government")]

Some countries present military and civilian decorations known as Silver Medals. These include:

* [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria")′s [Silver Medal for Services to the Republic of Austria](/wiki/Silver_Medal_for_Services_to_the_Republic_of_Austria "Silver Medal for Services to the Republic of Austria")
* [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy")′s [Silver Medal of Military Valor](/wiki/Silver_Medal_of_Military_Valor "Silver Medal of Military Valor")
* [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa")′s [Silver Medal for Merit](/wiki/Silver_Medal_for_Merit "Silver Medal for Merit")
* The [Civil Air Patrol](/wiki/Civil_Air_Patrol "Civil Air Patrol")′s [Silver Medal of Valor](/wiki/Silver_Medal_of_Valor "Silver Medal of Valor") in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").

## Other awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Other awards")]

The [Zoological Society of London](/wiki/Zoological_Society_of_London "Zoological Society of London") awards a [Silver Medal](/wiki/Silver_Medal_(Zoological_Society_of_London) "Silver Medal (Zoological Society of London)") "to a Fellow of the Society or any other person for contributions to the understanding and appreciation of zoology, including such activities as public education in natural history, and wildlife conservation."

The [Royal Academy of Engineering](/wiki/Royal_Academy_of_Engineering "Royal Academy of Engineering") awards a Silver Medal "for an outstanding and demonstrated personal contribution to UK engineering, which results in successful market exploitation, by an engineer with less than 22 years in full-time employment or equivalent."

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Silver_medal&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Runner-up](/wiki/Runner-up "Runner-up")
